key_insight: Random sampling maps provide a unified way to compare and compress inputs of different sizes, enabling explicit guarantees for generalization and sketching in variable-size learning problems.
engineering_relevance: This gives practical guidance for designing scalable models and preprocessing pipelines for sequences, graphs, point clouds, and tensors when training and inference sizes differ.
method: Define sampling notions that generalize replacement, random binning, and species sampling.
Match each sampling type to the symmetries/relations of the domain.
Derive generalization and sketching rates for function classes continuous under the chosen sampling notion.
evidence_signal: The paper is theoretical and derives explicit rates and domain-specific examples rather than reporting a new empirical benchmark.
watch_item: The framework depends on selecting the correct sampling notion for the domain, so a mismatched choice may weaken the guarantees or make implementation nontrivial.
